title: The Drama
review: Our past will always haunt us…and our past will define us…to some extent. Choosing to forgive or not, and what draws the lines: no right answers for each person/ couple, but we definitely all wish for grace, whether we give it or not.

title: Poor Things
review: An aggressive ask on Progressivism, free will, social construct, and patriarchal system: the concept of shame and how it is attached to sex. Experimental attempt.

title: Left-Handed Girl
review: Intimate perspective and lovable narrative on a recurring pattern (premarital pregnancy). Also depicts how preferences on sons influence asian family support system and bonds. Another angle is how female strive and survive in cornered situation, facing struggles, with anger and lament, but also with an attitude of unyield.

title: American Primeval
review: Depicts early American settler’s conflicts between each other and different native tribes. Adopted from the 1857 Mormon Meadows Massacre, the series depicts the cruel and deadly situation to survive in the western parts of the states and early morman’s way of entering Utah.

title: Il Gattopardo (The Leopard)
review: A grand reproduction of the novel adapted, famously made film by visconti. The new series were able to capture more detailed and visually narrated story lines that were loss in Visconti’s version.

title: Squid Game

title: Man On Fire
review: A lot of close ups and shaky takes to portray Creasy’s emotional state. However, a black guy found the meaning of life from a white little girl, killed gangs and her father, and in the end says he’s going back home with a mountain scene? (tbh I relate it to another continent with a major mountain, where his ancestors home could be ) Tbh is cheesy and felt like a feel-good movie that tries to reconcile with the black people by bridging them with innoc

title: A Time To Kill
review: For a movie released in the 90s, maybe it was nice. Today it was still a feel-good movie for the white educated. Maybe it did pictured the way it was but most characters were plain and only supportive. Like, ok we get it Mathew is hot and he got two hot women who both liked him and he in the end did the right thing and retreated seduction, and we want to be him, right?

title: The Quick and The Dead
review:
A modern twist on traditional western: I see this movie as an epitome of feminism: LOTS of feminist analysis could be found here: ways of revenge, anxiety, despair, self-doubt, creativity, destroy, reconstruct, sexual desire and freedom. Also very interesting that the girl shot her own father dead in the beginning - could this be a male perspective’s redemption?  
In fact, not just female, you can see native american and black american roles, challenging white male supremacy.
